_source: NEC Article 690, NABCEP PV Installation Professional standards, Solar engineering handbooks
_note: Long-tail solar energy knowledge. System sizing, code compliance, inverter selection, battery storage calculations.

load_calculation.daily_energy_consumption: Sum all loads × hours used; kWh/day
load_calculation.example: Refrigerator 200W × 24hr = 4.8 kWh; lights 500W × 5hr = 2.5 kWh; total = 7.3 kWh/day
load_calculation.safety_factor: Multiply by 1.25 for inefficiencies (inverter, wiring, temperature)
load_calculation.seasonal_variation: Calculate for worst-case month (lowest sun hours)

solar_resource.peak_sun_hours: Equivalent hours of 1000 W/m² irradiance per day; varies by location and season
solar_resource.orientation: South-facing optimal in northern hemisphere; tilt angle ≈ latitude for year-round; latitude +15° for winter
solar_resource.shading: Use shading analysis; 10% shading can reduce output 50%+; micro-inverters help with partial shading

array_sizing.formula: Array size (kW) = (Daily energy need in kWh) / (Peak sun hours × System efficiency)
array_sizing.example: 7.3 kWh/day ÷ (5 peak sun hours × 0.77) = 1.9 kW array
array_sizing.module_quantity: Array size / module wattage; 1.9 kW / 0.35 kW per module = 6 modules (round up)

voltage_calculations.voc_temperature_correction: VOC increases in cold; multiply by temperature coefficient factor
voltage_calculations.max_system_voltage: VOC × temp factor × number of modules in series ≤ inverter max voltage
voltage_calculations.example: Module VOC 48V, temp factor 1.14 (-10°C), 10 in series: 48 × 1.14 × 10 = 547V; must be < inverter max (typically 600V residential)
voltage_calculations.vmp_operating: Nominal operating voltage; check inverter MPPT range; all operating conditions must fall within range

current_calculations.isc_array: Sum of string ISC if parallel; check < inverter max input current
current_calculations.imp_array: Operating current; used for wire sizing
current_calculations.continuous_current: ISC × 1.25 per NEC 690.8(A)(1); wiring and OCPD sized for this value

string_configuration.series_modules: Increases voltage; fewer strings; thinner wire; typically 8-14 modules in series for residential
string_configuration.parallel_strings: Increases current; more combiner complexity; balance strings (same # modules, orientation, tilt)
string_configuration.mismatch_loss: Different string lengths or orientations cause power loss; avoid if possible

wire_sizing_nec_690.ampacity_correction: Further multiply by 1.25 if in conduit with >3 current-carrying conductors; temperature correction factor
wire_sizing_nec_690.example: ISC 9A → 9 × 1.25 = 11.25A; in conduit × 1.25 = 14.06A; 14 AWG rated 20A × temp factor 0.91 = 18.2A; 14 AWG adequate
wire_sizing_nec_690.voltage_drop: Keep <2% for max power; V_drop = (2 × K × I × L) / CM; K = 12.9 copper
wire_sizing_nec_690.grounding: Grounded systems: EGC sized per 250.122; ungrounded: PV bonding conductor per 690.43

inverter_types.string_inverter: One inverter for entire array; lowest cost; shading affects all modules; central MPPT
inverter_types.microinverter: One per module; module-level MPPT; best for shading; higher cost; AC coupled from roof
inverter_types.power_optimizer: DC-DC converters per module + string inverter; module-level optimization; mid-cost
inverter_types.hybrid_battery: Includes battery charger/inverter; grid-tied with backup; ESS (Energy Storage System)

sizing_criteria.dc_array_wattage: Total module wattage; inverter DC input typically 80-120% of this (oversizing up to 20% common in sunny climates)
sizing_criteria.ac_output: Inverter AC output ≥ max load (grid-tied) or critical loads (off-grid)
sizing_criteria.voltage_range: Array VOC (cold) < inverter max; array VMP range within MPPT window across all temperatures
sizing_criteria.current_limit: Total ISC × 1.25 < inverter max DC input current

efficiency.peak_efficiency: 96-98% typical; occurs at ~50% of rated power
efficiency.cec_weighted_efficiency: Better metric; weighted across power levels; use for comparison
efficiency.mppt_efficiency: 99%+ for quality inverters; tracks maximum power point

article_690.690_7_max_voltage: Max system voltage based on coldest expected temperature; table or calculation
article_690.690_12_rapid_shutdown: Shutdown to 80V within 30 sec inside array boundary, 30V outside (2017+); module-level or inverter-based
article_690.690_13_disconnect: Disconnect for PV system from premises wiring; lockable, labeled 'PV System Disconnect'
article_690.690_31_methods: Wiring methods: MC4 connectors + USE-2/PV wire exposed; conduit (EMT, PVC) for buried or building penetrations

ocpd_requirements.fuse_sizing: Overcurrent protection if >2 strings in parallel; sized 1.25 × ISC to 1.5 × ISC; use PV-rated fuses
ocpd_requirements.breaker_backfeed: If connecting to load side of service, breaker must be at opposite end of bus from main; 120% rule applies
ocpd_requirements.120_percent_rule: Main breaker + PV breaker ≤ busbar rating × 1.2; example: 200A bus, 200A main → PV breaker ≤ 40A

grounding.equipment_grounding: All metal frames, racking bonded; EGC to main panel
grounding.system_grounding: Functional ground (one conductor grounded) or ungrounded system (both ungrounded with GFDI)
grounding.pv_module_grounding: Listed module-level bonding; or use WEEB devices; maintains listing

labeling_requirements: Rapid shutdown label at service equipment
Diagram showing PV source circuits, inverter, disconnect, OCPD

lead_acid.flooded: Cheapest; requires maintenance; venting; 50% DOD typical
lead_acid.agm_gel: Sealed; no maintenance; better cycle life; 2× cost of flooded

lithium_ion.advantages: 80-90% DOD; 3000-6000 cycles; higher efficiency (95% vs 80%); lighter
lithium_ion.cost: 2-3× lead acid upfront; lower lifecycle cost ($/kWh over lifetime)
lithium_ion.bms: Battery Management System required; balancing, temperature, SOC monitoring

sizing_calculations.example_lithium: 10 kWh rated × 0.9 DOD × 0.95 efficiency = 8.55 kWh usable
sizing_calculations.days_of_autonomy: Typically 1-3 days for grid-tied backup; 3-5 days off-grid
sizing_calculations.formula: Battery Ah = (Daily load Wh × Days autonomy) / (System voltage × DOD × Efficiency)

charging.charge_stages: Bulk (constant current to 80%), Absorption (constant voltage to 100%), Float (maintenance)
charging.voltages_12v_lead_acid: Bulk 14.4-14.6V, Absorption 14.4-14.8V, Float 13.2-13.5V; varies by temp and chemistry
charging.temperature_compensation: -30mV/°C for lead acid; lithium less sensitive

nec_706_ess.706_15_disconnects: Disconnect means required on both line and load sides of ESS
nec_706_ess.706_21_terminal_marking: Positive and negative clearly marked; DC warning labels
nec_706_ess.706_30_batteries: In approved enclosure; ventilated if needed; accessible for maintenance; not in living spaces (lead acid)

grid_tied.operation: Exports excess to grid; draws from grid when production insufficient; no backup unless hybrid
grid_tied.net_metering: Credits for export; policies vary by utility; some pay wholesale rate, some retail
grid_tied.anti_islanding: Inverter must shut down if grid fails (UL 1741); prevents back-feeding grid during outage
grid_tied.interconnection: Utility approval required; typically requires disconnect, insurance, inspection

off_grid.components: PV array, charge controller, battery bank, inverter (may be combined)
off_grid.sizing: Must meet all loads including surge; battery for nights and cloudy days; often needs generator backup
off_grid.charge_controller: MPPT (higher efficiency) or PWM (cheaper, limited use); sized for array ISC × 1.25
off_grid.generator: Backup for extended low-sun periods; size for largest load + battery charging

hybrid_grid_tied_battery.operation: Grid-tied with battery backup for critical loads during outage; automatic switchover
hybrid_grid_tied_battery.critical_loads: Subset of loads on separate panel; typically <5 kW; fridge, lights, internet, outlets
hybrid_grid_tied_battery.modes: Self-consumption (use solar, charge battery, export excess), backup (grid outage), time-of-use arbitrage
hybrid_grid_tied_battery.cost: Highest initial cost; most flexibility; eligible for ITC (30% federal tax credit)

key_metrics.performance_ratio: Actual output / Theoretical output; accounts for all losses; >75% is good, >80% excellent
key_metrics.capacity_factor: Actual annual energy / (Array size × 8760 hours); 15-25% typical
key_metrics.specific_yield: kWh/kWp/year; normalizes for system size; compare to nearby systems
key_metrics.availability: % time system operational; >98% for quality systems

troubleshooting.underproduction: Check shading, soiling, wiring losses, inverter clipping, module degradation
troubleshooting.inverter_errors: Ground fault (check EGC), AC/DC disconnect open, over/under voltage (check grid), over temperature (check ventilation)
troubleshooting.module_issues: Hot spots (IR scan), microcracks (EL test), potential induced degradation (PID; check grounding)
